question_html:
Read the passage carefully and then answer the following questions: 10

Education aims to bring about positive changes in our behavior. It helps us try to change our lives as well as the society we live in. Education that does not illuminate our minds or does not inspire us to work for the community is not complete at all. Education is not all about getting grades or receiving certificates we use education to make life better. We are expected to apply the knowledge, skills and values that we learn in a classroom in our engagement with the world that lies outside. And we can do so in many different ways. Let's have a look at how education works.

Learners' civic engagement is highly appreciated all over the world. Civic engagement means working to make a difference in the civic life (the public life of the citizens as contrasted with private or personal life) of the community using knowledge, skills, values and motivation. Civic engagement promotes the quality of life in a community by contributing to the improvement of health and wellbeing of the people.

Civically engaged individuals recognize themselves as members of a larger social community and are concerned about civic issues. They consider themselves responsible citizens who take action or raise their voices against injustice, discrimination and other forms of social ills.

In civic engagement, issues of public concern are crucial. Civic engagement includes individual or group activities to protect public interests and change the way the community values itself. For example, if you are protesting against any unjust decision of the local municipality such as raising taxes or an act that might work against people's interests, you are civically engaged and your action is considered a civic engagement.

This protest can be done in different ways such as organizing rallies, collecting signatures, making human chains, writing petitions, etc. You can also help control traffic in front of a school, help children to cross the roads, work in a team to clean a park or a sea beach. You can also give some services to elderly people.

These are all examples of civic engagement as what you do directly affects the community.

When civic engagement is a part of an academic programme, and the learners' engagement is assessed following a scale, then it is called service learning. It involves the application of knowledge and skills learned in the classroom and then making a complete plan of action i.e. preparing a budget, starting the process of implementation, involving people and activating operational strategies. When civic engagement is spontaneous but irregular and not a part of any academic programme, it is considered volunteerism.

(a) “Education is not all about getting grades or receiving certificates.” Do you support this sentence? Why? Give reasons behind your answer.

(b) “Civic engagement promotes the quality of life.” Elucidate.

(c) How can you raise voice against an unjust decision?

(d) Do you think volunteerism should be a part of academic education? Why/why not?

(e) Identify the part of speech of the bold words and write their noun forms.
solution_html:
(a) Yes (0.5 Marks), I support this sentence because education is meant to bring positive changes in our behavior and society, not just grades or certificates. True education inspires us to work for the community and apply our knowledge, skills, and values in real life. It helps us improve our own lives and contribute to the wellbeing of others through civic engagement (1.5 Marks).

(b) Civic engagement contributes to the improvement of health and well-being of people. It also helps a person to apply the knowledge and skills learned in the classroom. This is how civic engagement promotes the quality of life (02 Marks).

(c) I can raise my voice against an unjust decision by organizing rallies, collecting signatures, making human chains, or writing petitions. These actions help express public concern and work towards protecting community interests (02 Marks).

(d) Yes, I think volunteerism should be a part of academic education as civic engagement and volunteer work help students apply their knowledge and skills to real-life situations (01 Mark). It also teaches them responsibility, teamwork, and concern for the community’s well-being (01 Mark).

Instructions:

(i) If the student writes a number instead of another (Ex. “a” instead of “b”), 0.5 marks have to be deducted from each of the numbers.

(ii) Individual marking must be done {Ex. a---0.5, b—0.5, etc.)}.

 (iii) Highest marks can be given if there are no mistakes (information, spellings and grammars) ----08

(e) Here are the parts of speech and the noun forms of the bold words:

Marks Distribution:

For identifying 04 parts of speech of the bold words (0.25 * 4 = 01 Mark) has to be given and for writing their adjective forms, Students will get (0.25 * 4 = 01 Mark).

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Benefits and Negative Aspects of Ecotourism’ within 10 sentences. 10
solution_html:
Benefits and Negative Aspects of Ecotourism

Ecotourism has numerous benefits, including economic support for local communities, conservation of natural habitats, and the promotion of environmental education. Local communities benefit economically through job creation in areas like guiding, hospitality, and transportation. Ecotourism also raises awareness about the need to conserve nature and the importance of protecting endangered species. This form of tourism helps preserve local ecosystems by creating financial incentives for conservation. Moreover, it often encourages the development of sustainable infrastructure and practices. On the downside, poorly managed ecotourism can have negative impacts, such as habitat disruption, pollution, and the overuse of natural resources. If not regulated properly, ecotourism can lead to the degradation of fragile ecosystems and wildlife disturbances. Overcrowding in popular destinations can also dilute the benefits of ecotourism, leading to environmental strain. There is also a risk that tourism may prioritize profit over environmental and cultural preservation. Thus, the balance between sustainable development and tourism is crucial for ensuring the long-term success of ecotourism.

Marks Distribution:

Highest Marks ----------------------------------------------09 Marks

(A) For Title (Benefits and Negative Aspects of Ecotourism) -------------------------------------01 Mark

(B) Topic Sentence (Introduction) -----------------------01 Mark

Ecotourism has numerous benefits, including economic support for local communities, conservation of natural habitats, and the promotion of environmental education.

(C) Context Explanation (Benefits and Negative Aspects of Ecotourism) (Any 6 Points) ----06 Marks
‎• Local communities benefit economically by creating jobs in areas like guiding, hospitality, and transportation.
‎• It promotes conservation of natural habitats and raises awareness about the importance of protecting endangered species.
‎• Financial incentives from ecotourism help preserve local ecosystems and encourage sustainable infrastructure development.
‎• Poorly managed ecotourism can lead to habitat disruption, pollution, and overuse of natural resources, negatively impacting fragile ecosystems.
‎• Overcrowding at popular destinations may dilute the benefits of ecotourism, causing environmental strain.
‎• There is a risk of prioritizing profit over environmental and cultural preservation.

(D) Conclusion -----------------------------------------------------------------01 Mark

In conclusion, the balance between sustainable development and tourism is crucial for ensuring the long-term success of ecotourism.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. We will consider up to 12 sentences. However, if a student writes more than 12 sentences but less than double (20 sentences), 50% of the deserving marks must be deducted. If it becomes double (20 sentences), full marks have to be deducted. Comment: Write within 10 sentences.

3. Students must write within a para. If a student writes more than one para, half of the deserving marks must be deducted. (Comment: Write within single/one para).

4. For writing only the title of the paragraph bears no mark. (comment: Write the Title / Benefits and Negative Aspects of Ecotourism)

question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with the following words: 10

Symbol, Ambitious, Disintegrate, Hostile, Resolution, Treacherously, Desolate, Separate, Intrinsic, Vulnerable
solution_html:
Symbol (Noun) = The dove is a universal symbol of peace, love, and hope for humanity.

Ambitious(Adjective)=The government has announced an ambitious plan to modernize the railway network.

Disintegrate (Verb) = Many meteors disintegrate during their passage through the atmosphere.

Hostile (Adjective) = The security forces exercised great restraint by not responding to hostile attacks and threats.

Resolution (Noun) = The legislature has approved a resolution calling for the removal of such advertising.

Treacherously (Adverb) = The soldier was attacked treacherously by someone he had once called a friend.

Desolate (Adjective) = After the storm, the once lively village looked desolate and completely abandoned by life.

Desolate (Verb) = She had been desolated by the death of her friend.

Separate (Adjective) = The twins were placed in separate rooms to prevent them from copying each other.

Separate(Verb) = The teacher separated the noisy students into smaller groups to maintain classroom discipline.

Intrinsic (Adjective) = Graduates are attracted to jobs that have intrinsic interest.

Vulnerable (Adjective) = The sudden resignation of the financial director put the company in a very vulnerable position.

Instructions:

(i) Any correct and logically meaningful sentence will be accepted. (Sentences with weak meaning will not be allowed.)

(ii) For any grammatical or structural mistake, full marks have to be deducted. Examiners have to show and correct the mistake(s) in the scripts.

(iii) For 02 spelling mistakes, 01 mark will be deducted, for 01 spelling mistake, 0.5 marks will be deducted from that very sentence.
‎(iv) For changing the part of speech of the given word by adding a suffix or prefix, the full marks have to be deducted from the very number. But students can use any form if the given word is a verb (depending on the subject-verb agreement, tense, person, number, etc.)
‎Comment: Use the given word without changing its parts of speech.

question_html:
Add six more sentences to complete the following story: 10

A king deemed a parrot foolish for lacking education. Ignoring its natural songs and flight, he ordered, “Educate the bird” .......
solution_html:
The Parrot's Tale

A king deemed a parrot foolish for lacking education. Ignoring its natural songs and flight, he ordered, “Educate the bird.” The task of educating the bird fell on the king’s nephews, his sisters’ sons. The learned men of the court deliberated long. They pondered the reasons behind the ignorance of the creature in question. After a long discussion, the conclusion was that, the bird’s nest made of straw and twigs could not hold much knowledge. The first thing needed was a proper cage. A gilded cage was made by a goldsmith. Then a teacher came to give lessons to the bird. He took a pinch of snuff and declared, “This isn’t a matter of just a few texts.” One of the royal nephews sent for scribes. They made multiple copies of various texts until there was a veritable mountain. After that the process of educating the bird was going on with the fullest spirit. Predictably, the bird weakened with every passing day, and lay half dead. The tutors considered it a good sign. One day the bird died. When it died nobody knew. The rumour went down and reached to the king’s ear. Then the king called his nephew and asked about the bird. The nephew replied that the bird education has been completed. Hearing this, the king ordered to bring the bird to him so that he could see that. The bird came. Along with it came the inspector, a guard, a cavalry officer. The king pressed the bird; it made no sound, not even a squeak. Only its stomach let out the rustling noise of dry pieces of paper.

Marks Distribution:

Highest mark---------------09 Marks

(i) Title (Any logical one) -------(01 Mark has to be deducted for not writing the Title)

“The Parrot's Tale”

(ii) Story (minimum new 6 sentences) ------1.5×6=09 Marks

{If a student adds or writes at least 6-8 new sentences using the given part and completes the story with a proper description without any grammatical or spelling mistakes, he/she will get full marks}.

Instructions:

(i) If a student doesn’t write the given part, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the given part first).

(ii) Students can write the story in any logical way (sample answer isn’t fixed).

(iii) The given part tells us that the story is the incidents of the Past. So, it is normal that the student will develop the story using Past Tense but it can be described in Present Tense and future Tense also by using direct speech.

(iv) If a student doesn’t write the Title, 01 Mark has to be deducted (comment: write the Title/ The Parrot's Tale)

(v) If a student writes fewer than five sentences, 01 mark will be deducted for each of the missing sentences. (Comment: Add at least six new sentences).

question_html: Explain the proverb in five sentences: “A stitch in time saves nine.” 5
solution_html:
This proverb ‘A stitch in time saves nine,’ captures timely actions. It means dealing with problems early, when they are small and manageable. Ignoring them allows them to foster and grow, making them much harder and more time consuming to fix later. It is a call-to-action to address issues promptly, like that tiny tear. A small effort now can prevent a much bigger headache down the line.

Mark Distribution:

(A) Theme of The Proverb-----------------01 mark
‎• The proverb captures timely actions.

(B) Context of The Proverb (A stitch in time saves nine) (03 points) -----03 marks

• It suggests that addressing small problems early prevents them from escalating into bigger issues.
‎• Ignoring minor issues can lead to more significant, time-consuming fixes later on.
‎• The phrase serves as a call to action for prompt problem-solving, likening it to repairing a small tear.

(C) Conclusion-----------------------------------01 mark
‎Ultimately, A little effort now can avert much larger difficulties in the future.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. We will consider up to 06-07 sentences. However, if a student writes more than 07 sentences, 01 mark has to be deducted from the deserving number.

Comment: Write within five sentences.

question_html:
The sentences in the following text are jumbled. Rearrange in a proper order: 5

(a) She attended the University of Wisconsin where she received her Bachelor of Arts degree in English.

(b) In 1933, the couple divorced. In 1941, she married Norton Baskin, a hotel owner from Florida.

(c) She married Charles Rawlings, also a writer.

(d) Rawlings was considered a writer of local or regional literature which focuses on a particular geographical setting of rural, Southern USA.

(e) Marjorie Kinnan Rawlings was born on August 8, 1896, in Washington, D.C.

(f) Although her stories are based on the seemingly simple lives of rural people, her themes are universal.

(g) A writer from an early age, she won a prize of $2 for a short story published in the Washington Post in 1907

(h) Rawlings continued to write until her death.

(i) The couple moved to New York, where they worked as journalists for various newspaper.

(j) She died in 1953.
solution_html:
Instructions:

 If a student writes only a matching/rearranged order, he/she will get full marks. Writing a full passage is not mandatory.

 If the students make a wrong matching, automatically another matching sentence may be wrong.

 If the students use other than a given number format (a, b, c, d…) in the matching row, 01 mark has to be deducted from the deserving marks.

 For every 3 grammatical or spelling mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted. (Depending on the student's matched full sentence writings)

question_html:
Translate the following Bangla paragraph into English: 5

সৌন্দর্যকে উপলব্ধি করা সহজ, কিন্তু সংজ্ঞায়িত করা কঠিন। আমরা যখন চারদিকে তাকাই, তখন আনন্দদায়ক বস্তু এবং দৃশ্যের মধ্যে সৌন্দর্য খুঁজে পাই—প্রকৃতিতে, শিশুদের হাসিতে, অপরিচিতদের উদারতায়। কিন্তু একে সংজ্ঞায়িত করতে বলা হলে আমরা সমস্যায় পড়ি। সৌন্দর্যের কি কোনো স্বতন্ত্র, বস্তুনিষ্ঠ অস্তিত্ব আছে? এটি কি সর্বজনীন, নাকি আমাদের ইন্দ্রিয়গ্রাহ্য অনুভূতির উপর নির্ভরশীল?
solution_html:
Translation: Beauty is easy to appreciate but difficult to define. As we look around, we discover beauty in pleasurable objects and sights - in nature, in the laughter of children, in the kindness of strangers. But asked to define, we run into difficulties. Does beauty have an independent objective identity? Is it universal, or is it dependent on our sense perceptions?

Instructions:
‎ For accurate translation using grammar, punctuation, and sentence structure, students will get full marks. [05 marks for translating 05 sentences correctly]

 For every 03 grammatical/spelling errors 01 mark has to be deducted.

question_html:
Re-write the following text with correct punctuation: 5

I will wait a while he said and then I will go. Where do the trucks go?
‎Towards Barcelona I told him.
‎I know no one in that direction he said but thank you very much. Thank you again
‎very much.
solution_html:
“I will wait a while,” he said, “and then I will go. Where do the trucks go?”
‎“Towards Barcelona,” I told him.
‎“I know no one in that direction,” he said, “but thank you very much. Thank you again
‎very much.”

General Instructions for punctuation marks:

(i) Full marks can be given if there are no mistakes / errors in this part.

 For 1 error ---------------01 mark has to be deducted.

 For 05 error or more……………full marks have to be deducted.

(ii) Marks have to be given based on errors not on correct ones. The examiner’s main focus will be on errors not on the correct ones.

(iii) If the student adds or subtracts any word, that every word will be counted as an error and the marks will be deducted according to the above system.

(iv) The examiner has to correct the mistake(s) in the script.

(v) Both “--”/‘--’ formats are correct as quotation marks.

(vi) “--”/‘--’ will be treated as 01 punctuation mark.

question_html:
Fill in each gap of the following passage with appropriate word(s) from the basket given below. Make any grammatical changes (if necessary). 5

Children's night to education is widely (a)________ today as a fundamental human right. But that right also implies (b)_______ the school they go to will have a pleasant and learning-friendly environment where everyone will have an (c)_______ time. Teachers will be kind, caring and supportive and children will feel (d)________. No harsh words will be spoken to them and special care will (e)________ of children with learning disabilities.
solution_html:
(a) recognized

(b) that

(c) enjoyable

(d) relaxed

(e) be taken

Instructions:

Any kind of mistake bears no mark. But mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with suitable words. 5

Relationships are (a)_______ different kinds. Some are familial and intimate, (b)_______ by blood and by marriage; some are (c)_______ like the ones we have with friends and some are made in schools where we form close (d)_______ with classmates and teachers. Relationships can also be fostered in workplaces, which may (e)________ change from professional to social.
solution_html:
(a) of

(b) formed

(c) social

(d) bonds

(e) quickly

Instructions:

Any kind of mistake bears no mark. But mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Transform the following sentences as directed. 5

(a) Every rose has a thorn. (Negative)

(b) Buy one shirt and get one free. (Complex)

(c) Where there is life, there is hope. (Simple)

(d) By moving, he will die. (Compound)

(e) His conduct annoyed me. (Passive)
solution_html:
(a) There is no rose without thorn.

(b) Buy one shirt so that you can get one free. / Buy one shirt if you want to get one free.

(c) There is no life without hope.

(d) Let him move and he will die.

(e) I was annoyed at his conduct.

Instructions:

(i) For any kind of grammatical mistake (i.e. subject, verb, tense etc.), full marks have to be deducted.

(ii) For missing Question Mark /Exclamation Mark in sentence 0.5 marks have to be deducted from each deserving marks.

(iii) In case of spelling mistakes,

* For 1/2 errors----0.5 marks have to be deducted.

* For 03 errors------01 mark has to be deducted.

N.B. Alternative answers can be accepted.

question_html: Write the theme of the poem ‘Those Winter Sundays’ by Robert Hayden within five sentences. 5
solution_html:
The poem explores the theme of unspoken parental love and sacrifice, highlighting how acts of care often go unnoticed or unappreciated, especially by children. It reflects on the emotional distance within families and the regret that can come with recognizing love too late. The speaker realizes that love is not always expressed through words, but often through quiet, selfless actions.

Marks Distribution:

Highest Mark--------------------------------------05

(A) The Theme of the Poem ---------01 Mark The poem explores the theme of unspoken parental love and sacrifice.

(B) Context Explanation (Those Winter Sundays) (Any 4 Points) ---------(01 × 04) = 04 Marks
‎• It highlights how the parents' acts of care and selfless devotion frequently go unnoticed or are unappreciated by their children.

• The text reflects on the existence of emotional distance and disconnect within the family dynamic.

• The speaker experiences regret upon recognizing the depth of the love and sacrifice only too late.

• The core realization is that true love is expressed through quiet, selfless actions rather than just verbal affirmations.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. We will consider up to 06-07 sentences. However, if a student writes more than 07 sentences, 01 mark has to be deducted from the deserving number.

Comment: Write within five sentences.
